questions.question: As per the recent notification from the Union Ministry of Home Affairs (MHA), which industry has been exempted from the lockdown?
questions.explanation: The Union Ministry of Home Affairs (MHA) has recently released a notification, wherein it has exempted the operations of Fishing (Marine) and Aquaculture Industry from the nation-wide lockdown imposed to curb the spread of coronavirus. The operations of the marine and aquaculture industry including harvesting, processing, packaging, cold chain, sale and marketing; hatcheries, movement of fish/shrimp etc. were exempted in the order. The order also directed that social distancing and proper hygiene practices must be ensured.

questions.question: M S Mahabaleshwara has been reappointed as the MD and CEO of which bank, for three more years?
questions.explanation: India’s leading Private sector lender Karnataka Bank announced that it has received approval from the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) for reappointment of Mahabaleshwara M S as its MD and CEO for next three years, with effect from April 15. The Reserve Bank has also approved the reappointment of P Jayarama Bhat as Non-executive Chairman up to November 13, 2021.

questions.question: The Central Bank of which country has become one of the investors with more than one per cent stake in Housing Development Finance Corporation Ltd (HDFC)?
questions.explanation: The Central Bank of China, the ‘People’s Bank of China (PBoC)’ has joined the group of investors with more than one per cent stake in Housing Development Finance Corporation Ltd (HDFC). People’s Bank of China now holds 1.01 per cent in HDFC, which is the largest standalone housing finance company of India. The Central Bank has been included in the group of 8 foreign portfolio investors (FPIs), who own more than one per cent stake in HDFC.

questions.question: Chief Information Commissioner and all Information Commissioners have decided to take a 30% voluntary cut in their basic salaries. Who is the Chief Information Commissioner of India?
questions.explanation: The Chief information commissioner of India, Bimal Julka and all 6 information commissioners have decided to take 30-per cent cut in their basic salary for a year starting April 2020, to contribute to government’s move in fighting COVID-19. In March 2020, President of India Ram Nath Kovind administered the oath of office to Bimal Julka as the CIC of India. At present, four positions of information commissioners are remaining vacant. The Chief Election commissioner and Election commissioners will also undergo a 30% pay cut.

questions.question: TLTRO route, which was making news recently, is associated with which institution of India?

options.label: A
options.text: Securities and Exchange Board of India

questions.explanation: The Reserve Bank of India has recently announced that the banks that have availed funds under the first tranche of the targeted long-term repo operations (TLTRO) route, will have to deploy the funds raised within 30 days. If the banks fail to deploy funds within the stipulated time from the auction, they shall have to pay a penal interest of an additional 200 basis points (bps)in the policy repo rate, for the number of days these funds are not deployed.

questions.question: Which term is used to define the difference between the total imports of the country and the total exports?
questions.explanation: Trade Deficit is defined as the difference between the total imports of the country and the total exports. This condition occurs when a country is importing more goods than it is exporting. Recently, the Commerce Ministry announced that the trade deficit in the month of March fell to $9.76 billion, which is the lowest in the last 13 months. Due to the steep fall in export of leather, gems and jewellery and petroleum products, the total exports in the year 2019-20 decreased.

questions.question: The Indian women team of which game has acquired a direct berth to participate in the World Cup 2021?
questions.explanation: The International Cricket Council has recently announced that the India women’s cricket team has acquired a direct berth to participate in the ICC Women’s Cricket World Cup 2021 The World Cup event is scheduled to be held in New Zealand from February 6 to March 7 in 2021. With 23 points, Indian team has joined the group of 5 teams along with New Zealand, Australia, England and South Africa, which have been already qualified.

questions.question: As per the recent report of the United Nations, over 117 million children around the world would miss the vaccine for which disease, amid the Covid-19 pandemic?
questions.explanation: As per the recent report of the United Nations, over 117 million children in 37 countries around the world would miss to receive the measles vaccine, amid the coronavirus pandemic. According to the World Health for Organization and the UNICEF, the immunisation campaigns for eliminating measles have already been delayed in 24 countries and more campaigns will be postponed. WHO has released guidelines to sustain the immunisation process in several countries.

questions.question: Researchers have discovered a creature, which is claimed to be the world’s longest animal, in which country?
questions.explanation: Researchers have recently discovered a creature, which is claimed to be the world’s longest animal, in the underwater canyons of Western Australia. About 30 new marine species have also been discovered in the marine region off the Australian coast. Though the exact length of the siphonophore has not yet been measured, it is estimated that the unidentified flying object (UFO)-shaped creature would be more than 120m long.

questions.question: Matterhorn mountain, which was seen in news recently, is situated in which continent?
questions.explanation: The Matterhorn mountain is a famous mountain located in the Alps range with a height of 4478 meters. It is one of the highest summits of the Alps and spans between two European countries Switzerland and Italy. The summit was seen in news recently, as a famous Swiss light artist Gerry Hofstetter lit up the mountain with the Indian flag to express their solidarity for India, in fighting the Covid-19 pandemic. The summit has been illuminated with the flags of various countries for nearly 3 weeks, as a part of nightly series.
